title: ranunculus lepidulus
intro: Ranunculus lepidulus, also known as the small-flowered ranunculus or lesser buttercup, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and glossy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus lepidulus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, Ranunculus lepidulus can be found in infusions, tinctures, and salves, often used to treat skin conditions and promote wound healing.
